title: Stocks
content: Stocks represent ownership in a company. They generally offer higher long-term growth potential but also higher short-term volatility compared to bonds or cash equivalents.

title: Bonds
content: Bonds are fixed-income investments where investors lend money to governments or corporations in exchange for periodic interest payments and principal repayment at maturity.

title: Mutual Funds
content: Mutual funds pool money from many investors to buy a diversified portfolio of assets. They are priced once per day after market close.

title: Index Funds
content: Index funds aim to track the performance of a specific market index, such as the S&P 500. They are commonly used for low-cost, diversified, passive investing.

title: Asset Allocation
content: Asset allocation is the mix of stocks, bonds, cash, and other assets in a portfolio. The ideal allocation depends on risk tolerance, age, financial goals, and time horizon.
